summary: There is no upper limit on the functional fraction of the human genome because even once-considered non-functional elements like pseudogenes play active roles in regulating gene activity via regulatory RNAs affecting mRNA stability and expression. The expanding understanding of genomics reveals functionality across diverse sequences including those previously labeled junk DNA.
